item.title: West Ham co-chair quits ahead of ‘publication of serious historic allegations’
item.description: David Sullivan departs just two weeks after club was relegated from Premier League

item.title: Oil tanker owners fear market crash after Iran war drove record profits
item.description: Shipowners ploughed windfall into new vessels and are braced for steep drop in rates if Strait of Hormuz reopens

item.title: One billion pints: beer giants prepare for booziest World Cup in history
item.description: An expanded tournament held in beer-friendly countries will result in an extra 1bn pints being consumed, say analysts

item.title: How Monaco changed for F1
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: The $10bn club, pickleball’s rise and more

item.title: Peru to vote on ninth president in a decade
item.description: Neither candidate, who offer widely differing agendas, obtained 20% in first round of voting

item.title: Can AI save a company’s soul?
item.description: It is easy to imagine institutional memory lost to automation preserved in silicon — but there is one crucial difference

item.title: AstraZeneca chief warns group could withhold new drugs in Europe
item.description: Sir Pascal Soriot says countries will have to spend more on innovative medicines following US trade deal

item.title: Investors wake up to the merits of sleep biotech
item.description: More people worry about poor sleep than excess weight, but drugmakers have been deterred from entering the market by costs

item.title: On yer bike! Freud and middle-aged men in Lycra
item.description: The ever-present fear of ridicule among cycling’s weekend warriors tells us a thing or two about human nature

item.title: Irish government under pressure over alumina exports to Russia
item.description: Lawmakers call for EU sanctions on Oleg Deripaska-linked plant in Ireland if raw material is used in Russian weapons

item.title: In World Cup season, choose your financial trophies carefully
item.description: Real Madrid’s president has made it very clear that investing in the club is mainly about having fun and making contacts

item.title: Wasting China’s solar panel surplus is madness
item.description: Clean power is within our reach — yet factories sit idle

item.title: Directors’ Deals: BP veteran hives off nearly £2mn in shares ahead of Manifold brouhaha
item.description: The group has been struggling to convince the market that it now possesses greater strategic clarity

item.title: How inheritance tax prompted the great pensions rethink
item.description: With the rules changing next year, savers need to reconsider what they do with their ‘red money’

item.title: Police in England and Wales told to halt AI use in court statements
item.description: Safeguards must be in place before forces automate justice tasks, says head of Police.AI

item.title: Time’s up for Swatch?
item.description: A fall in profits has increased pressure for change, but the Hayek family has decried ‘short-termism’ and defended its strategy

item.title: Working abroad? Here’s how not to fumble your UK pension
item.description: Changes to UK tax rules mean it’s more important than ever to plan ahead

item.title: Ten numbers you need to know if you’re planning your retirement
item.description: Pension planning is less straightforward than it used to be — data can help when uncertainties abound

item.title: UK first-time buyers turn to Grandpa and Grandma for financial help
item.description: Relatives other than parents have become a key source of funding for younger buyers, study finds

item.title: Top Goldman lawyer Ruemmler to remain with bank despite Epstein ties
item.description: General counsel resigned after revelations about relationship with disgraced financier but now will stay on as adviser

item.title: SpaceX signs $30bn deal to lease computing capacity to Google
item.description: Agreement comes ahead of a record-breaking initial public offering for Elon Musk’s rockets-to-AI conglomerate

item.title: Nasdaq tumbles 4% as shares in chip and memory groups sink
item.description: Rising expectations of a Federal Reserve rate increase send US bond yields rising sharply

item.title: Inside the CBS mutiny against Bari Weiss and David Ellison
item.description: A saga that started with Trump’s assault on ‘60 Minutes’ has turned into a full-blown crisis

item.title: Trump says US may take equity stakes in AI companies
item.description: President suggests ‘partnership’ will ease voter concerns about the technology ahead of November’s midterm elections

item.title: European airlines sit on €3.2bn in unpaid delay compensation
item.description: FT calculations show just a fraction of €18bn due since 2011 has been paid out

item.title: Meta weighs big equity raising after blockbuster Google deal
item.description: Facebook parent could sell tens of billions of dollars in new stock as it seeks to finance AI infrastructure

item.title: Parents force Starmer into embracing social media ban
item.description: Prime minister set to announce restrictions this month despite previous opposition

item.title: The ethical dilemmas of AI
item.description: How we address the revolution is a question of how we manage its uncertainties

item.title: Raspberry Pi surges as investors bet on hardware linked to AI boom
item.description: UK maker of tiny low-cost computers expects ‘robust demand’ to push unit sales above 4mn in first half

item.title: Could you be the FT’s new stock picking champion?
item.description: Register your portfolio now — the 8-week competition starts on 9 June
